name: Interleukin-1 receptor antagonist (IL-1Ra)
glycosylation: none (anakinra is non-glycosylated; bacterial E. coli production OK)
mechanism: Competitive antagonist at IL-1R1; blocks IL-1alpha and IL-1beta signaling. Same receptor as canakinumab target but receptor-side rather than ligand-side.

anakinra_clinical.off_label_gout: Used off-label for acute gout flares unresponsive to NSAIDs/colchicine; meta-analyses support; not FDA-approved for gout
anakinra_clinical.comment_on_cmax: Cmax range 1.3-1.8 ug/mL across PK studies; central estimate 1.5

soluble_IL_1R2_decoy.mechanism: IL-1R2 is a decoy receptor; soluble form binds IL-1beta with high affinity but lacks signaling intracellular domain
soluble_IL_1R2_decoy.evidence_level: In Vitro / Animal Model — Symons 1995, Reddy 2007
soluble_IL_1R2_decoy.vs_il1ra_for_flare: Higher affinity for IL-1beta specifically but does NOT block IL-1alpha (which IS released during MSU/NLRP3); for gout-flare specifically IL-1Ra preferred for dual-blockade

il1beta_binding_nanobody.mechanism: VHH single-domain (12-15 kDa) against IL-1beta — analog of canakinumab without Fc
il1beta_binding_nanobody.evidence_level: Mechanistic Extrapolation — no clinical program for gout
il1beta_binding_nanobody.vs_il1ra_for_flare: Smaller payload (cheaper mRNA), but blocks only IL-1beta not IL-1alpha; canakinumab clinical precedent shows IL-1beta-only is sufficient for gout but at $300K/yr — does not change the modality math

alternative_il1_axis_payloads.selection: IL-1Ra. Reasoning: (1) anakinra clinical precedent gives a direct exposure benchmark to model against; (2) dual IL-1alpha + IL-1beta blockade is the gout-flare-relevant mechanism; (3) 17 kDa is well within mRNA-LNP expression-burden range; (4) non-glycosylated — alveolar epithelium glycosylation differences are not a concern.

route_selection.reasoning: (1) Lung surface area ~70 m^2 — largest absorptive surface; (2) inhaled mRNA-LNP has the deepest CDMO + clinical-trial infrastructure (CF, RSV programs); (3) self-administration via nebulizer or DPI matches the home-flare-management use case better than SC injection (anakinra fails on patient adherence partly because injection at flare onset is unpleasant); (4) pulmonary expression -> systemic distribution via the alveolar capillary bed delivers IL-1Ra into the same plasma compartment that anakinra reaches via SC depot, with comparable or faster onset; (5) intra-articular requires one device per flare per joint and assumes the patient knows which joint will flare hours in advance.
limitations_acknowledged: Systemic IL-1Ra distribution from pulmonary expression depends on alveolar -> capillary protein transit; the published precedent is for vaccine antigens (IM/IN), CF CFTR (cell-local), insulin (Afrezza, small molecule)
Lung-expressed IL-1Ra concentration profile is the model output, not a directly measured value

sources.anakinra_pk: Kineret prescribing information; Yang et al. 2003 PK summary; Granowitz 1992 single-dose PK
sources.anakinra_gout_offlabel: Janssen et al. 2019 Lancet Rheumatol meta-analysis; Ghosh et al. 2013
